doc: Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_min: return the smaller of two operands per IEEE 754-2008 `minNum` semantics — NaN propagates only when both are NaN; otherwise the non-NaN operand wins. Signed zero is ordered `-0 < +0` via a special pre-test (mpfr_cmp returns 0 for ±0 pairs regardless of sign). Class is 'arithmetic' (50ms per-case budget): the op is a constant-time dispatch on kind + one compare + a single roundToPrec helper call. The chosen operand is then rounded to the target prec via the standard mpfr_set-like padding/roundMantissa pattern.
c_signature: int mpfr_min(mpfr_t rop, mpfr_srcptr x, mpfr_srcptr y, mpfr_rnd_t rnd)
divergence_from_c: C mutates rop and returns ternary as int; TS takes prec positional and returns {value, ternary}. Behaviour matches the C reference exactly (mpfr/src/minmax.c L31–L57). IEEE 754-2008 minNum semantics: do NOT confuse with 754-2019 `minimum` which propagates NaN — MPFR follows the older contract. The both-zero pre-test is load-bearing because mpfr_cmp returns 0 for any ±0 pair; without it the dispatch would mis-route to whichever operand is `a`.
refs: mpfr/src/minmax.c L31–L57 — the C reference for mpfr_min.
src/internal/mpfr/cmp_raw.ts — substrate compareMPFR, returns null on NaN, 0 on ±0/±0.
src/internal/mpfr/round_raw.ts — substrate rounding for the prec-refit step.
src/ops/max.ts — direct sibling (compare branches and signed-zero polarity flipped).
src/ops/cmp.ts — public cmp surface; documents the signed-zero quirk.
src/core.ts — locked types; posZero / negZero factories.
CLAUDE.md 'Hallucination-risk callouts' — Signed zero is real (min(+0, -0) = -0); NaN ≠ NaN (IEEE 754-2008 minNum returns non-NaN); rounding-mode count is FIVE.
